source: round-4 market-breadth audit (wvsoph0ny); King ask: best sites in every market, broad, for multi-field fuel
note: 71 market template directions, all capture-safe, single-file. Each = a real best-in-class exemplar + its scroll=0 signature move. Build via _batch-workflow.js (reconstruct brief from signature). DROP the 2 webgl-dependent ones (jewelry-watches-webgl-scene, game-studio-world-portal) or do a no-WebGL variant. Mix axes/clusters per batch.

directions.name: InsurTech Conversational Quote Hero
directions.exemplar: Lemonade (lemonade.com)
directions.signature: Full-bleed lifestyle photo or short loop video opens scroll=0; a single embedded chat widget (not a form) floats center-screen with one pre-filled question ('Check our prices' or 'Get a quote in 90 sec') - brand color is warm/unexpected (coral/pink) rather than institutional blue, breaking category expectation on first glance
directions.whyTemplate: Not the fintech dark metric hero; InsurTech signature is the chatbot-as-hero replacing the traditional multi-field quote form - the scroll=0 grammar is conversational widget + lifestyle image, not product dashboard or metric wall

directions.name: Wealth Management Lifestyle Asset Hero
directions.exemplar: Wealthsimple (wealthsimple.com)
directions.signature: Scroll=0 is a single large 3-D-rendered or animated lifestyle object (golden coin, abstract form, dimensional material) against near-white canvas - zero navigation clutter, one serif or large humanist headline, one ghost CTA; the visual object IS the brand story, replacing charts, graphs, or product UI entirely
directions.whyTemplate: Distinct from both the SaaS dark metric hero (product UI screenshots) and the fintech trust-stack (compliance-first layout); wealth management premium signature is editorial lifestyle object + generous white space + no product UI at scroll=0 - targets high-net-worth retail, not developer audiences

directions.name: B2B Neobank Product Mosaic
directions.exemplar: Mercury (mercury.com)
directions.signature: Deep indigo-black (#171721) full-viewport hero with inverted white type headline; a tightly-cropped dashboard UI 'bento box' mosaic of product tiles (cards, transactions, analytics) fills the lower 40% of the hero at a slight perspective tilt - single coral/indigo pill CTA; no lifestyle photography at all
directions.whyTemplate: Retail banking is white/navy/trust-badges; this is the inverse - dark canvas, no stock imagery, product UI as the hero asset. Mercury/Brex/Ramp share this dark-product-mosaic grammar that reads immediately as 'startup OS for money', not consumer bank

directions.name: Retail Bank Trust Hierarchy
directions.exemplar: Lloyds Bank (lloyds.com)
directions.signature: Sticky primary-action bar (Log On / Open Account) pinned above a full-width hero band; below the fold, a three-column product card grid anchored by navy/green brand color blocks with FSCS/FDIC trust badge row at viewport bottom - every element ordered by conversion priority, never by brand storytelling
directions.whyTemplate: Covered fintech trust-stack is startup-dark-mode credibility; retail bank is the opposite convention - institutional white/navy, regulatory badge strip, sticky CTA bar, card grid - a genuinely different grammar serving community banks, credit unions, and regional lenders

directions.name: VC / PE Portfolio Gallery Authority
directions.exemplar: Sequoia Capital (sequoiacap.com) / Lux Capital (luxcapital.com)
directions.signature: Full-bleed editorial hero with a single conviction headline ('We help the daring build legendary companies'); immediately below: a dense logo/portrait gallery of portfolio companies as the proof wall - no product, no pricing, no form; the portfolio IS the product at scroll=0
directions.whyTemplate: VC/PE is neither B2B SaaS nor consulting - the page grammar is 'proof by association' (founder faces, logo grid, notable exits) not features/benefits. Reusable for fund marketing, family office, and angel syndicate sites that live or die on portfolio credibility

directions.name: Management Consulting Thought Leadership Editorial
directions.exemplar: McKinsey & Company (mckinsey.com) / Oliver Wyman (oliverwyman.com)
directions.signature: Scroll=0 is a full-width editorial magazine-cover layout: bold oversized sans headline (40-60px) over a high-contrast editorial photo or abstract graphic; below the fold a 3-column card grid of research reports, not services - the firm proves authority through published thinking, never through a services pitch or pricing CTA
directions.whyTemplate: Consulting is not B2B SaaS (no metric hero, no pricing page CTA at top), not law firm (no case wins), not VC (no portfolio grid) - it is pure editorial authority architecture where the content feed replaces the hero CTA, a distinct and reusable pattern for professional services advisory firms

directions.name: Law Firm Case Authority Dark
directions.exemplar: Quinn Emanuel (quinnemanuel.com)
directions.signature: Dark or near-black canvas at scroll=0 with a single high-stakes headline ('$X billion recovered' or 'The world's leading trial firm'); a global reach graphic or iconic landmark photography fills background at low opacity; award/ranking badges float at bottom - authority via outcome numbers, not team photos or services list
directions.whyTemplate: Law firm dark outcome-proof is distinct from consulting (editorial content feed) and B2B SaaS (product UI); the signature move is monetary/outcome metric as headline anchor on a prestige-dark canvas - reusable for litigation firms, big law, and legal-tech platforms targeting GC buyers

directions.name: Payroll / HR-Tech Human Warmth + Clarity
directions.exemplar: Gusto (gusto.com)
directions.signature: Warm off-white or mint canvas hero with a short declarative headline ('Payroll, HR, benefits. Simplified.') set in a rounded humanist sans; a flat or lightly-illustrated product UI card floats right of headline; no dark backgrounds, no metric walls - the palette and copy register as 'approachable ops tool' rather than enterprise software
directions.whyTemplate: HR-tech sits between consumer app warmth and B2B enterprise authority; Gusto/BambooHR signature is warm-palette + declarative simplicity + rounded illustration UI card, explicitly rejecting both the dark enterprise aesthetic and the cold corporate grid - distinct from B2B dark metric hero and enterprise SaaS

directions.name: B2B Enterprise Software Outcome Proof
directions.exemplar: Coupa (coupa.com)
directions.signature: Above-the-fold hero leads with a quantified ROI claim ('300% ROI in year one', '$X trillion in managed spend') set in heavy type; beneath it: a three-panel 'X-Factor' or feature-intersection graphic rather than a product screenshot; trust logos (Fortune 500 customer logos) appear in a scrolling strip before the first fold break - authority via scale numbers, not product UI
directions.whyTemplate: This is the enterprise spend-management / procurement pattern: quantified ROI claim as H1 + customer logo strip + capability intersection graphic. Different from SaaS dark metric hero (which shows live product UI) and B2B warm HR-tech (which leads with simplicity messaging); enterprise procurement sells to CFO/CPO buyers who need proof-of-scale first

directions.name: Commercial Real Estate Property Intelligence
directions.exemplar: JLL (jll.com) / Cushman & Wakefield (cushmanwakefield.com)
directions.signature: Scroll=0 hero alternates between a dramatic aerial/interior property photograph and a data-insight banner (market outlook headline + chart thumbnail); a horizontal 'service lanes' bar (Leasing / Capital Markets / Property Management / Research) spans full-width below the hero image as the primary navigation - image authority + data credentials in one frame
directions.whyTemplate: CRE is not residential property presales (already covered); the CRE brokerage pattern is aerial photography + market-data ticker/insight + horizontal service-lane nav, serving institutional clients (investors, corporate occupiers, REITs) rather than homebuyers - a distinct grammar with no current library coverage

directions.name: Accounting / Tax Firm Credential Grid
directions.market: Accounting, CPA, and tax professional services
directions.exemplar: Thompson Greenspon CPA (thompsongreenspon.com)
directions.signature: Clean white hero with professional team photography (not stock) above a five-column service card grid; an awards carousel or certification badge bar runs below the cards; color palette is conservative (navy, charcoal, white) with a single accent; trust is built through credentials density, not design flair - every pixel answers 'are you qualified?'
directions.whyTemplate: Accounting websites have a recognizable 'credential-first' grammar - awards carousel + service card grid + conservative palette + no hero animation - that serves CPA firms, tax preparers, and audit practices. Distinct from consulting editorial authority (which leads with thought leadership content) and legal dark outcome proof

directions.name: Mental Health Calm Canvas
directions.exemplar: Headspace (headspace.com)
directions.signature: Scroll=0: full-bleed soft gradient background (lavender, warm peach, sage), rounded character illustration (abstract blobs or friendly shapes, no sharp edges) centered or left-anchored, single reassuring headline in a heavy rounded typeface, one low-friction CTA ('Start for free' style). Zero medical iconography. Palette deliberately avoids clinical white.
directions.whyTemplate: Telehealth is a covered adjacent topic but no template covers the soft-illustration, character-driven calm pattern used by wellness apps. This is a distinct emotional register - the whole goal of scroll=0 is to remove anxiety, not project authority. Reusable across therapy, meditation, sleep, and behavioral health.

directions.name: Supplement 3D Product Stage
directions.exemplar: More Nutrition (Awwwards SOTD Nov 2025, moreprotein.com)
directions.signature: Scroll=0: near-fullscreen 3D render of one hero product (jar, pouch, capsule) centered on a tightly controlled 1-2 color backdrop (matcha green, cream, blush), frame-by-frame scroll animation baked to canvas so the product 'rotates' or 'pours' as the user scrolls. No nav clutter at top. Product name in oversized condensed display type overlaid on the 3D object.
directions.whyTemplate: More Nutrition won Awwwards SOTD November 2025 precisely for this scroll-driven 3D product staging pattern. The generic e-commerce PDP is already covered. This is a distinct market template: single SKU hero, DTC supplements, scroll=canvas animation. Different grammar from the covered product PDP.

directions.name: Humanoid Robotics Character Site
directions.exemplar: Fauna Robotics (faunarobotics.com) - Awwwards SOTD June 16 2026
directions.signature: Scroll=0: light off-white or pale blue backdrop, illustrated or 3D-rendered robot character centered as the hero (styled playful, not industrial - asymmetric shapes, warm accents), large rounded display type introducing the robot by first name ('Meet Sprout'). Character has intentional personality cues visible at scroll=0 (posture, color, mascot quality). No spec sheets above fold.
directions.whyTemplate: The existing esports team roster covers competitive/industrial character presentation. This is the inverse: humanizing autonomous machines for consumer coexistence contexts. Fauna Robotics (SOTD June 2026) defines the genre - approachable robotics brand pages that lead with character personality, not engineering specs. Reusable for any consumer robot, social robot, or home AI device launch page.

directions.name: Clinic Trust + Instant Booking
directions.exemplar: Maven Clinic (mavenclinic.com)
directions.signature: Above-the-fold: full-bleed warm neutral background, single dominant search bar or specialty-selector grid, soft brand color (sage, blush, warm green), sticky nav with one CTA button. No hero image of doctors in white coats - the search or booking widget IS the hero. Trust signals (certifications, real provider headshots) appear in a compact band immediately below.
directions.whyTemplate: Covered templates focus on fintech trust-stack and biotech investor briefs. No template yet captures the patient-acquisition flow for a local/virtual clinic: warm emotion-led color palette + booking-first layout + provider roster grid. Distinct from the generic e-commerce PDP because the decision unit is care access, not a product.

directions.name: Autonomous / Aerospace Cinematic Dark
directions.exemplar: Reliable Robotics (reliable.co) - Awwwards Honorable Mention; SpaceX (spacex.com) as genre anchor
directions.signature: Scroll=0: pure black or near-black full-bleed canvas, single large cinematic photo or video still of the vehicle/machine in flight or operation (high contrast, dramatic lighting), white uppercase condensed headline stacked tight (often D-DIN or a custom geometric sans), minimal nav. Mission statement in one sentence below the headline. No color accents - the only color is the vehicle itself against darkness.
directions.whyTemplate: SpaceX pioneered this grammar and Reliable Robotics (Awwwards HM) follows the same convention for autonomous aviation. Dark cinematic aerospace is a distinct market template with a signature visual grammar (black canvas, photo hero, tight condensed white type) that differs from the deep-tech editorial authority template (which is editorial-column, editorial-grid). Reusable for aerospace, defense, autonomous aircraft, satellite companies.

directions.name: Cleantech Carbon Credential
directions.exemplar: Climeworks (climeworks.com) and Watershed (watershed.com) as co-exemplars
directions.signature: Scroll=0: crisp white or pale warm-neutral background with a bold mission headline in dark type ('We remove CO2 from the air'), an immediate quantified proof point (certified tons removed, enterprise customers, or a live counter), and a two-path CTA split (individual buyers vs. enterprise). Facility photography or a subtle data diagram anchors the hero. No abstract gradients - data specificity IS the trust signal.
directions.whyTemplate: Climate tech has a defined and now-recognizable visual grammar: mission-first copy, real-world facility or nature photography, hard numbers as trust signals, dual buyer paths. Not covered in the existing library. Distinct from biotech investor briefs (which are capital-raise focused) and from deep-tech editorial. Reusable across DAC, renewable energy, carbon markets, ESG platforms.

directions.name: Longevity / Biometric Dashboard
directions.exemplar: Function Health (functionhealth.com) and Blueprint by Bryan Johnson (blueprint.bryanjohnson.com)
directions.signature: Scroll=0: dark or off-black background (contrasts with clinical white of conventional health), a personal biological age metric or biomarker panel rendered as a clean dashboard graphic in the hero (not photography), headline that quantifies the value proposition ('Test 100+ biomarkers', 'Know your real age'). Color accent is a single vibrant hue (electric blue, acid green) over the dark base. Feels more like a fintech dashboard than a hospital site.
directions.whyTemplate: Longevity/biohacking has grown into a distinct consumer category with its own visual grammar: dark-mode dashboard aesthetic, quantified-self data panels, anti-clinical brand voice. This is not the same as the SaaS dark metric hero (which is B2B tool-focused). This template serves consumer health optimizers. Bryan Johnson's Blueprint and Function Health both execute this pattern and neither reads as conventional healthtech.

directions.name: Dental Smile Gallery
directions.exemplar: Vivid Specialized Dentistry (vividsmile.com) and Forni Dental as genre benchmarks
directions.signature: Scroll=0: warm cream or soft blush background (not clinical white), oversized before/after smile close-up as the dominant visual (cropped tight to the smile, never a full face portrait), headline in refined serif or soft-weight sans ('Your smile, transformed'), and a prominent 'Book a consult' CTA. No equipment photos, no stock doctor imagery above fold. The smile result itself is the product hero.
directions.whyTemplate: Dental is a large, distinct practice category with a recognizable conversion-optimized pattern: the smile transformation as the primary trust signal, warm non-clinical palette to reduce anxiety, and high-proximity result photography. Not covered by any existing template. Distinct from the generic clinic booking template in that the transformation result (not the service or provider) is the scroll=0 hero.

directions.name: MedTech Precision Device
directions.exemplar: Intuitive Surgical (intuitive.com) - da Vinci 5 launch pages as genre anchor
directions.signature: Scroll=0: deep charcoal or very dark navy background, a single lit product photograph of the device (surgical robot arm, imaging system, wearable sensor) rendered with studio lighting precision, headline in clean neutral sans-serif stating clinical superiority or FDA clearance status, one CTA for clinical teams ('Request a demo' or 'Clinical evidence'). Product is shown in surgical or lab context - no patients, no doctors, just the machine.
directions.whyTemplate: Medical devices sell to clinical buyers (surgeons, hospital procurement) and demand authority through material precision and clinical evidence, not warmth. The scroll=0 pattern is: premium dark studio product shot + clinical headline + evidence CTA. This is a distinct B2B clinical template not covered by existing entries. The MedTech device page has a closer grammar to luxury product photography than to the clinic-booking template.

directions.name: Semiconductor Nanoscale Precision
directions.exemplar: ASML (asml.com)
directions.signature: Scroll=0: pure white or very pale cool-grey background, a single large macro or electron-microscope-style photograph of a wafer, chip die, or lithography pattern filling most of the viewport, headline in a clean geometric sans-serif that anchors the technology scale ('Making chips smaller, faster, greener'), navigation flat and corporate but typographically refined. The microscopic photography communicates precision at a scale ordinary photography cannot. No 3D renders - real photography of sub-nanometer objects.
directions.whyTemplate: Semiconductors sit at the intersection of advanced manufacturing and deep tech but have a visual grammar all their own: the extreme macro/micro photograph of the physical chip or wafer as the hero, a clean white engineering feel (not dark like aerospace), and B2B technical authority messaging. ASML's site is the genre-defining exemplar. Not the same as the deep-tech editorial template (editorial grid, article-first) or the MedTech device template (studio product shot on dark). Reusable for chip companies, photonics, materials science.

directions.name: Veterinary Warm Pet Welcome
directions.exemplar: Animal Hospital of Sandy Springs (ahss.com) and Oceanside Veterinary Clinic as genre benchmarks
directions.signature: Scroll=0: vibrant but warm hero photograph of a pet (dog or cat, joyful, close-up), background in a complementary brand color (warm coral, sage, friendly purple), clinic name in a bold rounded sans-serif, single line of emotional copy ('Where pets feel at home'), prominent 'Book Appointment' button. Vet photography always features the animal, not the vet staff. Logo mark is often a geometric animal silhouette.
directions.whyTemplate: Veterinary practices have a distinct emotional register - the purchase trigger is pet love, not personal health anxiety. The pattern is: animal-first photography, warm emotional color, rounded approachable type. This differs meaningfully from the human clinic booking template in emotional tone, photography subject, and brand voice. Colorlib and Beyond Indigo Pets document this as a consistent convention across the best-in-class vet sites.

directions.name: Space / Launch Mission Countdown
directions.exemplar: Rocket Lab (rocketlab.com) and Relativity Space (relativityspace.com) as genre exemplars
directions.signature: Scroll=0: full-bleed video loop or still of a rocket on a launch pad or in orbit against pure black, countdown or mission status indicator in monospace type at top or bottom of frame, headline minimal ('We Make Space Accessible'), navigation ultra-minimal and flat. The scale of the rocket against the darkness of space communicates mission magnitude without words. Orbital footage or CGI at cinematic quality.
directions.whyTemplate: The aerospace cinematic dark template covers autonomous vehicles and defense; this is a sub-genre with its own specific scroll=0 signature: rocket + launch pad + countdown or mission tracker, evoking the livestream event feel. Rocket Lab and Relativity Space both execute a mission-narrative page that differs from generic dark-cinematic by the specific launch-event grammar (countdown, mission name, orbit destination). Distinct enough from the aerospace template above to warrant its own direction for the space vertical.

directions.name: Luxury Fashion Editorial Commerce
directions.exemplar: Loewe.com (redesigned by Work & Co) and Jacquemus.com
directions.signature: Gutterless full-bleed editorial grid: no product card borders, no gutter between images; campaign photography fills every cell edge-to-edge at scroll=0, with a persistent right-rail configurator (size/color/material) that never collapses even as the editorial scroll continues
directions.whyTemplate: Existing library has 'SaaS dark metric hero' and 'e-commerce PDP (generic)' but nothing for luxury fashion's specific grammar: editorial-first, commerce-second layout where the page reads as a magazine spread before revealing itself as a shop. The gutterless grid + right-rail PDP is a reusable commerce pattern distinct from standard e-commerce PDP.

directions.name: Beauty Skincare Ritual Editorial
directions.exemplar: Aesop.com
directions.signature: Typographic-first scroll=0 hero: a single large-serif headline (the product 'formulation philosophy' or ritual name) over warm sepia/cream background, zero product image above fold; product grid appears below with ingredient callouts as first-class UI labels rather than description copy
directions.whyTemplate: Inverts the standard beauty e-commerce pattern (big product photo hero) to lead with philosophy/ritual language then earn the scroll. Aesop's HOW-International-Award-winning redesign established this pattern that has spread to Grown Alchemist, Vitner's Daughter, etc. Not in library. Distinctly different from generic e-commerce PDP.

directions.name: Luxury Jewelry & Watches Immersive Scene
directions.exemplar: Cartier Watches & Wonders 2025 by Immersive Garden (Awwwards SOTD, Agency of the Year 2025)
directions.signature: Six discrete 3D WebGL 'rooms', one per hero watch; user navigates between rooms via horizontal gesture; each room is a fully atmospheric scene (lighting, texture, ambient audio) built around the object - no UI chrome except a minimal progress dot row; the watch is the only interactive element
directions.whyTemplate: Cartier has won Awwwards SOTD for this format three consecutive years (2023, 2024, 2025, 2026), making it the canonical exemplar for the jewelry/watches market. The room-by-room scene navigation is a distinct template grammar not represented elsewhere in the library. WebGL flag honest: this is the genre's signature - a non-WebGL degraded version is viable and still capture-safe at scroll=0 with a static hero shot.

directions.name: Bold-Color CPG Food Brand
directions.exemplar: Magic Spoon (magicspoon.com) and Graza (graza.co)
directions.signature: Oversized saturated flat-color background (one brand color dominates the full viewport), single product shot centered with playful packaging as the hero, press-logo bar ('As seen in') immediately below fold, and a 'Build Your Own Pack' or 'Pick Your Flavor' interactive row as the first commerce unit - no lifestyle photography, no models
directions.whyTemplate: Challenger CPG DTC brands (Magic Spoon, Graza, Olipop, Liquid Death) have converged on a distinct grammar: bold single-color hero + product packaging as art object + social proof bar + interactive pack builder. This is a recognizable and highly reusable template for the food/CPG DTC market, not present in the library.

directions.name: EV / Premium Auto Cinematic Hero
directions.exemplar: Polestar.com (designed by Code & Theory, Stockholm Design Lab) and Rivian.com
directions.signature: Full-viewport dark cinematic video hero (vehicle in motion, natural/outdoor environment), vehicle silhouette fills 80%+ of the frame, all UI reduced to a single headline in thin white type and one 'Configure / Reserve' CTA; zero lifestyle models, zero spec copy above fold; the car is the entire scroll=0 composition
directions.whyTemplate: EV and premium auto brands have converged on a specific cinematic-vehicle-as-art grammar (Polestar, Rivian, Lucid, Genesis) that differs from mass-market auto (feature spec tables) and from consumer electronics. The dark-tone, single-vehicle, configure-CTA pattern is a reusable template for any aspirational vehicle or high-ticket product launch.

directions.name: Sneaker Drop Launch Page
directions.exemplar: Nike SNKRS (snkrs.com) and StockX drop pages
directions.signature: Full-screen product hero image centered on a floating shoe with dramatic side lighting, single SKU focus, visible countdown timer to drop, raffle/notify CTA above the fold, and a 'sold out' / 'enter draw' state toggle - the entire page is a single conversion unit built around manufactured scarcity
directions.whyTemplate: Distinct from generic e-commerce PDP; the signature move is the countdown+raffle mechanic embedded directly in the hero as the primary UI element. This pattern is used across Nike SNKRS, Supreme, Palace, Kith, and is deeply specific to footwear/streetwear drop culture. Reusable for any limited-release product vertical.

directions.name: Niche Fragrance Scent Narrative
directions.exemplar: Byredo.com
directions.signature: Single-column centered layout with a maximally spare bottle photograph floating on a white field, product name in oversized lowercase type above it, and a poem-length scent story as the only body copy - no navigation clutter, no grid, no price above the fold; the page reads as an art book page not a shop
directions.whyTemplate: Fragrance cannot be smelled through screens so the genre substitutes language and whitespace as sensory proxies. Byredo perfected this: the scent narrative is the product page hero. Le Labo, Diptyque, and Memo Paris follow the same grammar. This is a coherent, reusable template direction for any product where the emotional story must precede the buy decision.

directions.name: Craft Beverage / Wine Origin Story
directions.exemplar: BRAND Napa Valley (2025 WebAward Best Beverage Site) and specialty coffee roasters on elias.studio roundup
directions.signature: Split scroll at scroll=0: left half is a full-height map or terrain photograph (vineyard, farm, sourcing region), right half is a vertical text column with origin provenance copy and a single CTA; color palette is earth-tone or harvest-derived, never white; sensory storytelling leads, SKU listing appears at scroll depth 2
directions.whyTemplate: Craft beverage websites (wine, specialty coffee, single-origin spirits) share a specific terroir-first grammar that is distinct from generic food brand design. The provenance split-screen is immediately recognizable and reusable across any product with geographic/origin story marketing. Not in library.

directions.name: Design Furniture / Homeware Room Scene
directions.exemplar: HAY.com and New Works (cited by Shopify enterprise 2025 roundup as 'editorial furniture that blurs ecommerce and design journal')
directions.signature: Alternating full-bleed room-scene rows: product shown in situ in a styled room on the left, minimal spec + CTA column on the right; at scroll=0 a hero lifestyle image fills the viewport edge-to-edge with no UI elements except a transparent header; the first product is discovered only after scrolling past the room scene
directions.whyTemplate: Furniture and homeware brands (HAY, Muuto, Menu, Hem, Hem, Ferm Living) have a distinct scroll grammar: room scene before product detail, lifestyle leads commerce. Different from generic e-commerce PDP and from editorial-only layouts. Reusable template for any high-consideration home/decor product.

directions.name: Consumer Electronics Feature Reveal
directions.exemplar: Dyson.com and Sony product microsites
directions.signature: Sticky-scroll product feature reveal: the product (vacuum, headphone, camera) is pinned center-frame at scroll=0 on a near-black background, and as the user scrolls, labeled callout lines animate in to highlight individual features (motor, filter, sensor) one at a time while the product slowly rotates in place; the product never leaves the viewport until all features are revealed
directions.whyTemplate: The sticky-scroll feature-callout pattern is the canonical consumer electronics template (Apple established it, Dyson and Sony adapted it). No pure static electronics template in the library; this is a distinct, highly reusable format for any complex physical product with multiple differentiating features. Capture-safe: a single frozen frame with callout lines visible works as a static screenshot.

directions.name: DTC Subscription Personalization Funnel
directions.exemplar: Ipsy.com and Birchbox.com subscription landing pages; Graza and Calming Co buy-box patterns
directions.signature: Above-fold is a two-step quiz widget embedded directly in the hero section (not a separate quiz page): 2-3 multiple-choice questions ('What is your skin type?', 'What goals matter most?') with illustrated option tiles, then immediate personalized product reveal inline; subscribe CTA appears only after quiz completion with a savings callout ('Save 20% with subscription')
directions.whyTemplate: The quiz-as-hero pattern (zero-party data collection before product reveal) is the canonical DTC subscription acquisition grammar, with 40%+ conversion rates per Interact research. It is meaningfully different from both the generic e-commerce PDP and the SaaS metric hero. Reusable for any subscription product vertical requiring preference capture before recommendation.

directions.name: Eyewear AR Try-On Commerce
directions.exemplar: Warby Parker (warbyparker.com) - 75% of iOS app users engage with AR try-on
directions.signature: Hero at scroll=0 is a face-frame split: left panel shows a real person wearing a frame (lifestyle/editorial photo), right panel is the same frame on a digitally rendered face with an 'Try On' CTA directly below; frame silhouettes in a tight horizontal scroll below the hero function as the category nav; no traditional product grid until below the fold
directions.whyTemplate: Eyewear DTC (Warby Parker, Ace & Tate, Clearly, Zenni) has a distinct try-on-first commerce grammar driven by the fit uncertainty problem unique to eyewear. The face-left / frame-right paired hero with try-on as primary CTA is not present in the library and is reusable for any wearable where fit/appearance-on-face is the purchase barrier.

directions.name: Game Studio World Portal
directions.market: Video game studio and game franchise promotion
directions.exemplar: Riot Games / Arcane microsite (Thinkingbox build, 2024-2025); Supercell.com game universe hub
directions.signature: The page IS a fragment of the game world: environment art fills the viewport at scroll=0, game UI chrome (health bars, crosshairs, or HUD elements) are repurposed as nav, and cursor movement triggers a subtle parallax depth shift - players recognize the aesthetic before reading a word
directions.whyTemplate: Unlike the library's 'esports team roster' (player cards / team stats), this is an IP/world-building template for the studio itself or a single franchise - the signature is world-immersion, not roster display

directions.name: Museum / Gallery Collection Explorer
directions.exemplar: Nordiska Museet (nordiskamuseet.se) - 2025 Webby Award winner, Awwwards Honorable Mention; Van Gogh Museum redesign by DEPT / Q42
directions.signature: A heritage-derived graphic motif (seal fragment, architectural detail, or archival mark) used as a dynamic framing device at scroll=0, with full-bleed artwork behind it - zero marketing copy visible; the first interaction is pure object encounter, not a call to action
directions.whyTemplate: Not in the library. Museum sites have a distinct grammar - object-first, zero hero text, collection as the hero - that differs entirely from gallery/portfolio or editorial authority templates

directions.name: Music Festival / Live Event Lineup Poster Grid
directions.market: Music festival and live event promotion
directions.exemplar: Primavera Sound (primaverasound.com) - design by Mucho, widely cited on Siiimple and Siteinspire; KIKK Festival (Awwwards-featured)
directions.signature: A constructive typographic poster system at scroll=0: the entire above-the-fold is the lineup rendered as an oversized typographic composition (artist names at varying weights and sizes forming a visual density map), no photography, no hero image - pure type as event
directions.whyTemplate: Not in the library. Festival sites are distinct from conference schedule templates (the library already has that) - the signature here is the lineup-as-poster aesthetic tied to music festival culture, not agenda/session grids

directions.name: Record Label / Artist Dark Immersive
directions.market: Record label and recording artist promotion
directions.exemplar: Deconstruction Records (awwwards.com/sites/deconstruction-records) and Sound of Change (Awwwards SOTD)
directions.signature: Full-viewport black canvas with a single looping audio-reactive visual (waveform, particle field, or animated vinyl art) at scroll=0; artist name in oversized high-contrast type; no chrome, no nav visible until hover
directions.whyTemplate: Existing library has 'music release hub' but that targets album drop campaign pages. This is a permanent label/artist identity template with audio-reactive hero - a distinct grammar that label rosters and solo artists need as a persistent home, not a campaign splash

directions.name: Film Studio / Production Company Cinematic Scroll
directions.market: Film production company and indie studio promotion
directions.exemplar: Not To Scale (notoscale.com) - Awwwards-recognized; full-site immersive animated director showcase
directions.signature: Full-bleed darkroom hero with a single autoplay reel or cinemagraph, studio name in editorial serif, scroll triggers episode-by-episode or project-by-project reveal with score audio fading in - the site is the trailer
directions.whyTemplate: The library's 'esports team roster' and 'deep-tech editorial authority' don't touch film production company identity. Film studios require a distinct cinematic reveal-on-scroll grammar tied to a reel, not a feature grid or roster

directions.name: Theatre / Opera / Ballet Layered Motion
directions.market: Performing arts venue (theatre, opera house, ballet company)
directions.exemplar: Nationale Opera & Ballet (operaballet.nl) - International Design Awards Gold 2024, German Design Award winner; Opera Ballet Vlaanderen identity by Pentagram
directions.signature: Choreographed typographic layers: a large performance title fragments and reassembles on load using the institution's bespoke display font, over a full-bleed stage or rehearsal still - motion mirrors the act of performance itself, not a banner or video reel
directions.whyTemplate: Performing arts venues have a unique constraint: programming changes weekly, so the hero must be typographic and data-driven, not static art. The layered-type-in-motion grammar is specific to this market and differs from film or music

directions.name: Pro Sports Club Live Dashboard
directions.exemplar: Juventus.com (Deltatre build) and NBA.com - consistently cited as best-in-class for high-contrast immersive sports design
directions.signature: Team color flood above the fold: full-viewport hero in primary team color with a player action shot bleeding edge-to-edge, overlaid with live match or season stats in the team's display typeface - score / standing / next match prominently above the fold as if it were a scoreboard
directions.whyTemplate: Not in the library. Sports clubs need a live-data-aware hero (score, table position, next fixture) as a first-class design element - a fundamentally different grammar from esports roster pages which focus on player identity cards

directions.name: Fashion Magazine / Editorial Luxury
directions.market: Fashion magazine and luxury editorial publication
directions.exemplar: Vogue.com / i-D (Vice Media) - Siteinspire editorial+fashion+luxury category leaders; frequently cited on Godly and Siteinspire for monochromatic editorial grid
directions.signature: A monochrome or duotone full-bleed cover image at scroll=0 with a single oversized editorial serif headline bleeding off the bottom edge - no sidebar, no nav bar visible, pure magazine-cover grammar where the image IS the interface
directions.whyTemplate: The library has 'e-commerce PDP (generic)' - fashion editorial is an entirely different template grammar focused on editorial storytelling and luxury brand positioning, not conversion. Cover-first layout with bleeding type is the specific signature

directions.name: Digital News / Magazine Editorial Grid
directions.market: Online news outlet and digital magazine
directions.exemplar: The Atlantic (theatlantic.com) and The New Yorker (newyorker.com) - repeatedly cited as 2025 best-in-class for editorial hierarchy web design
directions.signature: An asymmetric editorial card grid at scroll=0 where one lead story occupies 60-70% of viewport width with a full-bleed image and bold display headline, while a column of 3-4 secondary stories with small thumbnails fills the remaining third - print front-page hierarchy translated directly to web
directions.whyTemplate: Not in the library. News/magazine editorial grid is a distinct genre from blog, annual report, or API docs - the signature is the print-front-page hierarchy encoded in a responsive card grid, not a single hero or list view

directions.name: Book Publisher / Literary Press
directions.market: Independent book publisher and literary press
directions.exemplar: Penguin Books (penguin.co.uk) and Graywolf Press (graywolfpress.org) - cited on litmaglab and Colorlib 2025 roundups for typographic cover-grid design
directions.signature: A tight bookshelf-style cover grid fills scroll=0 - book jacket artwork in a dense mosaic with hover revealing title/author in serif type - no hero image, no banner; the catalog IS the homepage, with each cover as a clickable entry point
directions.whyTemplate: Not in the library. Book publisher sites have a unique bookshelf-grid grammar (cover art as the primary visual language) that is distinct from editorial magazines, e-commerce PDPs, or annual reports

directions.name: Podcast Network / Audio Brand
directions.market: Podcast network and audio content brand
directions.exemplar: Twenty Thousand Hertz (20k.org) - consistently cited as best-in-class podcast website design; Gimlet/Spotify Podcast network pages
directions.signature: A persistent sticky audio player docked to the bottom of the viewport is the hero affordance at scroll=0 - the latest episode auto-loads (not auto-plays) with waveform artwork, while a 3-column episode shelf with large artwork tiles fills the body - audio-first UI grammar where play is always one click away
directions.whyTemplate: Not in the library. Podcast networks have audio-player-as-nav as their defining constraint - this is a distinct template grammar from music release hubs (campaign-oriented) or news magazines (text-first)

directions.name: Creator / Influencer Personal Identity
directions.market: Content creator, influencer, and personal brand
directions.exemplar: Johnny Harris (johnnyharris.com) and Abi Connick - cited in 2025-2026 influencer website roundups for clean face-forward personal brand design
directions.signature: Full-viewport face photo or editorial portrait occupies scroll=0 left half, with name in a humanist sans at large scale on the right half and a single CTA (newsletter, collab, media kit) - the person IS the product; no reel, no testimonials, no features grid above the fold
directions.whyTemplate: Not in the library. Creator identity sites are fundamentally person-forward (the face is the logo, the voice is the brand) which is a distinct template from link-in-bio (utility aggregator) or portfolio (work showcase) - the scroll=0 grammar is portrait + name + one action

directions.name: Luxury Resort Cinematic Stillness
directions.exemplar: aman.com (redesigned 2024-2025, full-site art direction by And Partners NY) + Explore Primland (Awwwards SOTD Feb 4 2026)
directions.signature: Viewport-filling slow-pan video or single editorial photograph with near-zero UI chrome at scroll=0 — no booking widget, no banner, no price — only property name in refined serif and a single ghost-button CTA, using negative space as the luxury signal itself
directions.whyTemplate: Restaurant character-scroll (already in library) uses bold color and personality animation; this is its photographic-stillness counterpart for the premium hospitality market — minimal motion, maximum silence, poetic restraint as brand grammar rather than entertainment

directions.name: Destination Region Explorer
directions.exemplar: visitfinland.com (interactive animated region grid, progressive scroll disclosure) + traveloregon.com (full-page video hero with 'surprise me' randomizer)
directions.signature: Full-bleed atmospheric hero video or image at scroll=0, immediately below a responsive region/map grid where hovering a cell triggers a small animation and surfaces curated experiences — discovery as the primary UX metaphor rather than search
directions.whyTemplate: No existing library template covers the DMO (destination marketing organization) grammar: the region-grid explorer below an atmospheric hero is a repeatable convention used by national tourism boards globally, distinct from the generic travel e-commerce PDP already covered

directions.name: Architecture Firm Flush Project Grid
directions.exemplar: snohetta.com (feed-style living document, elegant white-range palette) + big.dk (bold full-bleed project grid, category hover overlay)
directions.signature: Viewport-filling masonry or uniform project-image grid that IS the homepage — no hero banner, no intro copy — project thumbnail tiles cover 100% of above-the-fold area, with project name and typology appearing only on hover
directions.whyTemplate: Differs from the deep-tech editorial authority template (long-form prose, dark background) and the SaaS metric hero (product UI) — the architecture grid follows a gallery-as-homepage grammar specific to project-portfolio practices where the work is the navigation entry point

directions.name: Creative Agency Reel-Expander
directions.exemplar: instrument.com (multidisciplinary agency, work-as-primary-nav) + Landscape agency (horizontal reel scroll, autoplay video modules) + Pentagram.com (industry-sorted work grid rebuilt by AREA 17)
directions.signature: At scroll=0 a looping showreel thumbnail occupies the dominant viewport zone; on scroll it expands to full-screen video; the work list IS the primary navigation — case study titles are the largest typographic element on the page, not the agency name
directions.whyTemplate: Agency reel-expander is a distinct grammar from the property-presales, SaaS metric hero, and devtools live-code hero already in the library; the showreel-as-homepage convention is endemic to creative studios and has no equivalent in the current template set

directions.name: Arts School Color-Slice
directions.exemplar: VCUarts (arts.vcu.edu) - 2025 Webby Award winner, designed by Fastspot
directions.signature: A narrow vertical color-slice runs the full height of the viewport on the right edge; as the user scrolls the slice cycles through a spectrum of department brand colors while a fixed left-rail vertical carousel surfaces department names with hover-reveal course imagery — identity system as scroll interaction
directions.whyTemplate: General university sites use editorial newspaper-grid or hero-with-search; VCUarts proves that art/design schools have a distinct convention — the color system IS the UX — which is reusable for any creative-education institution and is not covered in the existing library

directions.name: Nonprofit Scroll-Zoom Split
directions.exemplar: Farm Africa (farmafrica.org) - 2025 Webby Award nominee; Obama Foundation (obamafoundation.org)
directions.signature: Scroll=0 shows a bold single word or statistic at oversized scale over a human photograph; the first scroll gesture triggers a zoom-INTO the word/scene transition, then resolves into a fixed left-panel narrative anchoring the story while the right panel scrolls through supporting images and impact data — two-act reveal as the opening grammar
directions.whyTemplate: Distinct from the annual report template (data tables, fiscal summary) and link-in-bio; the scroll-zoom-split is the canonical convention for campaign-driven nonprofits communicating urgency and scale, with a reusable two-column anchored narrative structure not present in the library

directions.name: Civic Task-First Hero
directions.exemplar: City of Fort Wayne (fortwayne.gov) - 2025 Community Impact Award winner + 2024 NAGW Members Choice Award; Sacramento Regional Transit - 2025 Webby Award Best Government Site
directions.signature: Scroll=0 shows a city photography background with an oversized search or task-shortcut bar as the dominant element — 6 to 8 service shortcuts (Pay Bill, Report Issue, Permits, Events) rendered as large tile buttons occupy more visual weight than any branding or photography, prioritizing resident utility over civic image-making
directions.whyTemplate: Government/civic has a completely different conversion goal than every other market (task completion, not aspiration) and its resulting design grammar — task-dominant hero with utility shortcuts — is unlike any template in the library; the best examples have reached award-recognition level while maintaining accessibility compliance

directions.name: Place Brand Typographic Manifesto
directions.market: Place / City Brand (Promotional, not civic portal)
directions.exemplar: NYC Tourism updated five-boroughs rebrand (Print Magazine 2025) + City Nation Place award-winning destination brand campaigns
directions.signature: Scroll=0 is an oversized kinetic typographic statement of the place's identity claim (a single bold phrase in a bespoke city typeface) over a split documentary/editorial photograph grid — no map, no attractions list — the brand voice as pure type treatment is the entire above-the-fold
directions.whyTemplate: Place/city brand campaigns (I Amsterdam, Pure Michigan, NYC Tourism) have a distinct grammar from the tourism board region-explorer (which is utility/discovery-focused): this is the identity-launch / brand-manifesto format aimed at investors, talent, and media rather than vacation planners — different audience, different scroll=0 grammar

directions.name: Coworking Hospitality Editorial
directions.exemplar: industriousoffice.com (premium hospitality-register, sophisticated polished aesthetic) + Bad Company (strong community events grid, lived-in camaraderie)
directions.signature: At scroll=0 a warm editorial lifestyle photograph (people in conversation, not at desks) dominates the viewport with a hotel-grade color palette and minimal serif wordmark — no floor plans, no pricing table visible above the fold — membership tiers appear only after a second scroll, framing the brand as a curated social environment rather than an office product
directions.whyTemplate: Coworking is not covered in the library; its defining design convention (borrowing luxury-hotel visual grammar to sell desk space) is meaningfully different from SaaS, property developer presales, and hotel/resort templates already present — the hospitality-register applied to workspace is its own repeatable pattern

directions.name: Restaurant Group Concept Switcher
directions.exemplar: Cohn Restaurant Group (25 concepts, each with distinct identity) + FB Society (independent concept brands sharing a parent shell)
directions.signature: Homepage presents parent brand minimally at scroll=0; a horizontal strip of concept thumbnails acts as a visual nav — hovering a concept tile shifts the entire page palette, hero image, and typeface to that restaurant's identity in under 200ms — the page itself becomes the brand-switching UI
directions.whyTemplate: The restaurant character-scroll in the existing library is for a single restaurant's storytelling; this is the multi-concept group variant where identity-switching IS the product architecture — a distinct template serving hospitality holding companies, ghost-kitchen portfolios, and hotel F&B groups

directions.name: Logistics Route-Globe Hero
directions.exemplar: maersk.com (Maersk Design System, integrated container logistics) + Trans-Logistics (Awwwards MVP Honorable Mention Feb 2026)
directions.signature: At scroll=0 a stylized animated globe or network-route diagram occupies the right half of a split viewport, with key shipment-status or network stats (ports, countries, daily movements) as bold counters on the left — the network visualization communicates scale and trustworthiness before any copy is read
directions.whyTemplate: Logistics has no equivalent in the library; the route-globe split with live-counter stats is the industry's signature convention for enterprise-facing brand sites, clearly distinct from the status-page template (operational uptime) and the SaaS dark metric hero (product dashboards)

directions.name: EdTech Curriculum Path Map
directions.exemplar: Khan Academy (khanacademy.org, visual learning path trees) + Open edX (open.edx.org, modular course architecture displayed as visual maps)
directions.signature: At scroll=0 a branching curriculum tree or learning-path diagram rendered as a visual map dominates the hero — learner can see the full skill journey as a connected graph before enrolling, with completed/locked/available nodes color-coded — the map IS the product demo
directions.whyTemplate: The masterclass course PDP is already in the library but covers the premium single-instructor model; the curriculum-path-map is a distinct grammar for adaptive or structured EdTech platforms where progression architecture is the core value proposition — skill trees and learning maps are endemic to Khan Academy, Duolingo, and bootcamp platforms

directions.name: AI/ML Enterprise Authority
directions.exemplar: Cohere (cohere.com) - oversized warm-off-white hero, Voronoi-cell line-art iconography, and pastel glass cards with a single enterprise CTA
directions.signature: Oversized bold sans-serif headline centered on a warm off-white or near-black field; abstract generative background pattern (Voronoi cells, mesh, or diffusion swirl) at low opacity; trust logos row directly below the fold - no product screenshot in the hero itself
directions.whyTemplate: The existing 'SaaS dark metric hero' and 'AI reasoning-trace' templates serve PLG/dev-tool audiences. This is the distinct enterprise-AI variant: restrained palette (cream or near-black), NO visible product UI in the hero, abstract brand pattern instead - because enterprise buyers distrust flashy demos and respond to research-lab authority signals. Cohere, Anthropic, and Mistral all converged on this convention by 2025.

directions.name: Cloud/DevOps Blueprint Grid
directions.exemplar: Vercel (vercel.com) - monochromatic black-and-white, Geist typeface, subtle dot/line blueprint grid behind hero, accent glows for deploy states
directions.signature: Full-bleed black or near-black hero with a repeating fine grid of dots or orthogonal lines (blueprint aesthetic) stretching behind a centered headline; single white-on-black primary CTA; accent color only for system-state labels (deploy red, preview pink, develop blue) - no illustration, no gradient fill
directions.whyTemplate: Vercel pioneered a blueprint-grid convention that has spread to Neon, Supabase (dark variant), Railway, and Turso. It is architecturally distinct from the 'SaaS dark metric hero' (which is dashboard-screenshot-forward) - the blueprint-grid is typography-and-grid-only, evoking precision engineering. Highly capture-safe and zero WebGL.

directions.name: Cybersecurity Controlled-Dark
directions.exemplar: Wiz (wiz.io) and CrowdStrike (crowdstrike.com) - dark gradient hero with electric teal or orange accent, one animated risk-graph or cloud-map visual, understated glitch-free typography
directions.signature: Near-black to dark-navy gradient hero; ONE animated infrastructure/graph visual (cloud topology, risk graph, or heatmap) occupying the right two-thirds as a contained inset - not full-bleed; headline is clean sans-serif in white/light gray; single-color electric accent (teal, orange) applied only to CTAs and graph highlights - deliberate avoidance of glitch or terminal clichés
directions.whyTemplate: The category has matured past the stereotypical 'hacker red-on-black' into a controlled premium-dark visual grammar. Wiz, CrowdStrike, and Darktrace all use the same contained-graph + controlled-accent signature by 2025. Not covered by any existing template - closest is 'SaaS dark metric hero' but that is dashboard-centric; this is threat-visualization-centric with a distinct B2B security trust tone.

directions.name: Hardware/IoT Cinematic Scroll-Reveal
directions.exemplar: Apple product pages (apple.com/mac-pro, apple.com/airpods-pro) - scroll-driven image sequence reveals the physical product in 3D, with spec callouts appearing as the user scrolls
directions.signature: Above-the-fold: the physical product centered on a black or white void with cinematic lighting, zero UI chrome; scrolling drives a frame-by-frame 3D rotation or zoom using a preloaded image sequence (NOT WebGL); each new scroll position reveals a product angle paired with a one-line spec callout in small caps; no hero headline until 40% scroll depth
directions.whyTemplate: Apple's scroll-driven device-reveal pattern has become the canonical move for premium hardware (Teenage Engineering, Sony, DJI all follow variants). It is architecturally opposite to SaaS templates: the product is a physical object, the scroll IS the demo, there is no dashboard to show. At scroll=0 it is fully capture-safe (product on void). The pattern is widely imitated but not yet in the template library.

directions.name: Developer API Centered-Proof
directions.market: Developer API / infrastructure primitives (auth, email, realtime)
directions.exemplar: Clerk (clerk.com), Resend (resend.com), Liveblocks (liveblocks.io) - centered headline above a syntax-highlighted code snippet hero block, trust logos beneath, dark or white background
directions.signature: Centered layout; hero visual is a single syntax-highlighted code block (not a dashboard, not a diagram) showing the 3-5 lines needed to integrate the core feature; headline is a developer-register value prop ('Add auth in 10 minutes'); integration logo row immediately below; background is pure white or pure black - zero decoration
directions.whyTemplate: Evil Martians' 100-devtool study confirmed 'code snippet as hero' is the dominant pattern for API-primitive tools (auth, email, payments, realtime) - a distinct sub-genre from devtool product pages that show dashboards. The existing 'devtools live-code hero' in the library covers interactive REPL-style experiences; this template is the static, zero-interaction code-block-as-proof variant. Clerk, Resend, and Neon all use it.

directions.name: Design Tool Canvas-Alive
directions.exemplar: Framer (framer.com) - the marketing site IS the canvas; hero shows a live editable website frame inside the viewport with native motion, no static mockup
directions.signature: The hero viewport IS the product canvas: a real or simulated browser/editor frame fills 80-90% of the above-the-fold area, showing live content editing, cursor presence, or scroll interaction - the page markets by letting you feel the tool without a CTA
directions.whyTemplate: Figma, Framer, and Webflow all converged on 'the site is the product demo' as their scroll=0 move by 2025. This is distinct from devtool landing pages (centered headline + static screenshot) - here the product UI replaces the hero entirely. A strong reusable template for any creative/builder tool that needs to demonstrate fluidity before a user clicks anything.

directions.name: Productivity App Dark-Glass UI
directions.exemplar: Raycast (raycast.com) - dark background, floating product UI screenshot as hero object, real app-icon tiles in supporting rows, diagonal accent gradient band as launch motif
directions.signature: Deep purple-to-near-black gradient background; centered hero shows the full-fidelity product UI (floating command palette, inbox, or canvas) at near-native size with soft drop shadow - no device chrome, no lifestyle photo; real integration logos (Slack, Notion, GitHub) displayed as tile row below
directions.whyTemplate: Linear, Raycast, and Loom established a distinct convention: 'the product IS the hero object' with a dark ambient field. This differs from the SaaS dark metric hero (which uses dashboard screenshots inside a laptop/browser frame) - here the UI floats freely, frameless, treated like product photography. Reusable for any tool where the interface itself is the beauty proof.

directions.name: Fintech Infrastructure Paper-Trust
directions.exemplar: Plaid (plaid.com) - paper-currency-inspired guilloche line patterns, woodcut portrait illustrations, holographic-strip photography; calm blue palette with extreme whitespace
directions.signature: Extreme whitespace on a white or cream field; hero visual is an abstract currency-artifact motif (guilloche lines, engraving-style illustration, or connected-account flow diagram) NOT a product UI screenshot; muted blue/green single-accent palette; headline uses financial-weight language ('trusted infrastructure', 'powers X billion transactions')
directions.whyTemplate: Stripe and Plaid define a distinct fintech-infrastructure aesthetic that differs from the existing 'fintech trust-stack' template in the library (which is consumer-facing financial trust signaling). This template is API/infrastructure B2B: no dashboard UI, no consumer app screenshots - instead it uses currency-design motifs and institutional calm to signal regulatory trustworthiness at the infrastructure layer.

directions.name: Web3 Infrastructure Cosmic Gradient
directions.exemplar: Alchemy (alchemy.com) and Celestia (celestia.org) - dark field with a radiant multi-color mesh gradient or nebula bloom behind a developer-facing headline; chain-network node diagram as supporting visual
directions.signature: Full-bleed near-black background with a centered radial or mesh gradient bloom (purple-teal-blue or amber-purple) that feels like a space nebula; a simplified node-network or chain-block diagram sits in the lower half of the hero as the product visual; monospace or geometric sans headline; 'powered by X blockchains' badge row as social proof
directions.whyTemplate: Web3 infra sites (Alchemy, Hedera, Celestia, Cosmos) have converged on a 'cosmic dark with mesh gradient + chain diagram' signature that is distinct from generic SaaS dark and from the speculative/consumer crypto aesthetic. The gradient is not a UI gradient - it is ambient world-building that signals decentralized, planetary-scale infrastructure. No existing template covers this.

directions.name: No-Code Automation Flow-Canvas
directions.exemplar: Make (make.com, formerly Integromat) - animated workflow canvas showing connected app bubbles / node graph as the hero, with drag connections visually forming in the background
directions.signature: White or very light gray field; hero occupies 60-70% of viewport with a live or animated workflow node-graph (app logo bubbles connected by animated dashed-line arrows); the graph is not a screenshot - it is rendered as an interactive-feeling DOM canvas; headline and CTA sit in the left 30% in a left-split layout
directions.whyTemplate: Zapier, Make, and n8n all use a flow-graph-as-hero that is distinct from both the standard SaaS hero and the 'live-code hero' for devtools. The distinguishing move is the animated connection lines between brand-logo bubbles - it communicates 'everything connects' at a glance without any explanation. Not in the existing library and clearly reusable for any automation, iPaaS, or workflow product.

directions.name: Data/Analytics Live-Chart Hero
directions.exemplar: Amplitude (amplitude.com) and Hex (hex.tech) - light or dark hero with a prominent animated product chart or data grid as the hero visual, metric callouts overlaid
directions.signature: Light (white or off-white) or dark hero where an animated bar chart, funnel, or SQL-result table fills the hero visual area - NOT a full dashboard screenshot but a single chart widget extracted and displayed at display size; metric-callout badges (e.g. '+42% retention') float beside it as decorative overlays; headline positions the category (Product Analytics / Data Workspace) not a brand slogan
directions.whyTemplate: Analytics tools like Amplitude, Mixpanel, and Hex share a 'single chart as hero proof' convention that is distinct from the 'SaaS dark metric hero' (which uses a full-dashboard screenshot inside a device frame). The analytics variant isolates ONE chart object, animates it in isolation, and surrounds it with metric badges - a tighter, more focused proof-of-value move that fits a distinct template.

directions.name: Open-Source / Dev Community Hub
directions.exemplar: Hugging Face (huggingface.co) - warm yellow accent on white, emoji-driven social proof (star counts, model counts, contributor avatars), feed-style homepage showing live community activity
directions.signature: White background with a warm single accent color (yellow, orange, or green); hero leads with a community-proof stat block (stars, models, contributors, downloads) rather than a marketing headline; below the hero a live scrolling feed of recent community uploads, trending models, or starred repos renders as the primary content - the community IS the product proof
directions.whyTemplate: Hugging Face and Replicate have defined a 'community-is-the-product' homepage grammar that is wholly different from commercial SaaS or devtool pages. The hero's dominant element is community activity data, not a product screenshot. This maps to any open-source project, model hub, or dev community that derives credibility from contributor volume rather than enterprise logos.
